channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: HRCP urges urgent action for release of Pakistani crew held by Somali pirates
item.description: The Human Rights Commission of Pakistan (HRCP) has expressed serious concern over the ongoing captivity of Pakistani seafarers aboard the MT Honour 25, a Palau-flagged tanker seized by Somali pirates ...

item.title: Xpeng explores European production options with Volkswagen
item.description: Chinese electric vehicle (EV) startup Xpeng is exploring opportunities to establish a production footprint in Europe. According to a report by the Financial Times, the company is in discussions with V...

item.title: Building Long-Term Resilience in African Agriculture Amid Increasing Uncertainty
item.description: African agriculture is facing a rapidly changing and highly interconnected risk environment, where traditional pressures like rainfall, input costs, and supply chain challenges are now compounded by c...

item.title: Genetic Study Reveals a Third Ancestral Group in the Japanese Population
item.description: A groundbreaking genetic study conducted by researchers at RIKEN has revealed that the modern Japanese population descends not from two but three ancient ancestral groups. Previously, scientists belie...

item.title: Russian Billionaire Challenges Canada’s Sanctions Claiming No Ties to Putin
item.description: Nikita Anatolyevich Mishin, a Russian billionaire known for co-founding Russia’s largest train operator, Globaltrans, is suing the Canadian government to be removed from the sanctions list. Mishin arg...

item.title: AUKUS Submarine Plans Stalled as Costs Soar and Delivery Delayed
item.description: Australia's AUKUS submarine programme faces major setbacks as the government has already spent over $8 billion with another $2.8 billion planned next year, according to recent budget figures. A senior...

item.title: Rappler to Hold Forum and Workshops in Cotabato City to Prepare Voters for BARMM Elections
item.description: Rappler is set to visit Cotabato City from June 7 to 9, 2026, to educate voters and students about the upcoming historic Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) parliamentary elections...

item.title: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi Visits India for BRICS Talks, Highlights Minab School Incident
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in New Delhi on Wednesday to attend the BRICS foreign ministers’ meeting. Notably, his aircraft displayed the inscription 'Minab168', referring to the 1...

item.title: The History of Bell Confiscation and Repatriation in Germany During the World Wars
item.description: During both World War I and World War II, Germany and occupied territories faced large-scale confiscation of church bells, which were collected in so-called 'bell cemeteries' (Glockenfriedhöfe) before...

item.title: Manual Map and Blueprint Drafting Before Digital Tools
item.description: Before the rise of digital tools, drafting maps, architectural plans, and engineering designs required intense precision and patience. In the 1950s, draftsmen and engineers relied entirely on manual t...

item.title: Yobe APC commends party leaders for stepping down to maintain unity
item.description: Yobe State chapter of APC don show appreciation to key party members wey include Senator Ahmad Lawan, Senator Musa Mustapha, Alhaji Lawan Kolo Geidam, and Dr. Babagana Mohammed Adam after dem decide t...

item.title: Kenyan Woman Reflects on Challenges of Living Abroad and Desire to Return Home
item.description: A Kenyan woman living abroad has openly shared her frustrations with life outside the country, expressing boredom and emotional exhaustion despite being with her family. In a viral TikTok video, she d...

item.title: Study Shows Healthier Homes Can Significantly Reduce Malaria, Diarrhoea and Pneumonia in Children
item.description: A recent study in southern Tanzania has shown that simple improvements in house design can significantly reduce major childhood illnesses. Researchers introduced the 'Star home', a cost-effective and ...

item.title: Kenya Coastal Land Prices: Cheapest to Most Expensive Towns per Acre
item.description: The newly released HassConsult Coastal Land Price Index has ranked twelve coastal towns in Kenya by their average land prices per acre, revealing significant variations in value. Malindi emerged as th...

item.title: Understanding NEET UG 2026 Paper Leak and Comparison with China’s Gaokao System
item.description: Recently, there have been reports about a paper leak of NEET UG 2026 in India, raising serious concerns about the integrity of the examination system. NEET, being India’s largest medical entrance exam...

item.title: Treasury Reports Persistent Financial Mismanagement in Municipalities
item.description: Financial management in many South African municipalities remains poor, with unauthorised, irregular, and wasteful expenditure rising to R268.13 billion in the 2024/2025 financial year, up from R264.1...

item.title: David Tennant attends Rivals season two premiere in London with wife Georgia and daughter Olive
item.description: Actor David Tennant attended the London premiere of the second season of Rivals alongside his wife Georgia Tennant and their daughter Olive at the BFI IMAX cinema. The family appeared together on the ...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of 'The Magnificent Seven' (1960): Cast, Filming, and Legacy
item.description: ‘The Magnificent Seven’ (1960), directed by John Sturges, is a landmark American Western and a remake of Akira Kurosawa’s 'Seven Samurai'. The film relocates the story from feudal Japan to the Mexican...

item.title: Indian Ex-Army Chief Emphasises Dialogue and People-to-People Contacts with Pakistan
item.description: Former Indian Army Chief, Gen (Retd) Manoj Naravane, has openly supported remarks by RSS Secretary General Dattatreya Hosabale calling for sustained dialogue between India and Pakistan. In an intervie...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of Haley Joel Osment in “The Sixth Sense”
item.description: Haley Joel Osment, born on April 10, 1988, rose to fame as a child actor with roles in films such as Forrest Gump (1994), which earned him a Young Artist Award. His major breakthrough came with the 19...
